title: MD-1 medical droid

{{Quote|The ones we captured—two stormtroopers and all five of the transport's crew—refuse to talk. I have them in detention, isolated from each other. I've had an Emdee-oh and Emdee-one droid engaged in postmortems of the troopers we killed. With luck something will give us an idea where they came from.|Wedge Antilles reports on the situation following the Imperial raid on Talasea|<ref name="RS">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref>}}
The '''MD-1''' was a model of [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]] and a part of the [[MD-series medical droid/Legends|MD-series]] that was manufactured by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]]. It was slightly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] in appearance, possessed the ability to project [[Hologram/Legends|holograms]], and was specialized in laboratory chemical analysis.

==Description==
Manufactured by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]],<ref name="SWS" /> the MD-1 was a [[Class one droid/Legends|class one droid]]<ref name="Arms" /> that was part of the company's range of [[MD-series medical droid/Legends|MD-series]] of [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droids]].<ref name="SWS" /> Like all MD-series droids, the MD-1 resembled Industrial Automation's earlier design, the [[2-1B surgical droid/Legends|2-1B surgical droid]],<ref name="Droids" /> and was 1.5 [[Meter/Legends|Imperial standard meters]] tall. The unit consisted of a torso with two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and a cranial unit,<ref name="FF" /> and was bipedal<ref name="Arms" /> with lower half of the unit covered by a surgical gown to protect its internal mechanisms.<ref name="Droids" /> MD-1 droids were ambidextrous.<ref name="Arms" />

Units of the MD-series were generally covered in [[Color/Legends|gray]] plating with green highlights, and had yellow [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]].<ref name="FF" /> Costing 2,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] a unit, they were available for purchase throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Arms" /> The torso also contained a connection socket located on the lower half to allow the droid to directly connect to a [[Computer/Legends|computer]].<ref name="Droids" />

==Role==
MD-1 droids were designed as laboratory technicians, and were able to perform routine and advanced laboratory analysis on bacteria, germs, [[Poison/Legends|poisons]], and diseases. They were employed to develop vaccines and antibiotics.<ref name="SWS" />

==History==
Constructed on the [[droid factory]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Telti/Legends|Telti]],<ref name="Droids" /> they were employed by both the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="RS" /> Aboard the Imperial [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]'', an MD-1 droid oversaw the [[Medical bay/Legends|sick bay's]] laboratory.<ref name="FO">''[[The Far Orbit Project]]''</ref>

The Alliance's successor state, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], employed an MD-1 to assist a 2-1B surgical droid in performing an examination on [[Urlor Sette]], a former Alliance prisoner of war who was rigged to be killed by an internal device installed by [[Ysanne Isard]]. When [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] mentioned Sette's name, the internal mechanism was triggered, killing the man.<ref name="IR">''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]''</ref>

During the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] war, an MD-1 droid performed laboratory tests on the healing properties of the tears of the [[Fosh/Legends|Fosh]], [[Vergere]].<ref name="HT">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''</ref>
